independent_tests.test_command: pytest tests/test_assemble_tool_list.py tests/test_assemble_tool_list_layer3.py tests/test_prompts.py tests/test_subagent_prompts.py tests/test_subagents_jarvis_reasoner.py tests/test_supervisor_with_tools.py -v --tb=short

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py` (or wherever `assemble_tool_list` lives from FEAT-J002) gains an `include_frontier: bool = True` keyword-only parameter.
criteria_verification.evidence: Added `*, include_frontier: bool = True` keyword-only parameter to assemble_tool_list in src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py. Verified by tests TestAC001IncludeFrontierKeywordOnly: parameter exists, default is True, kind is KEYWORD_ONLY, annotation is bool, positional pass raises TypeError.
criteria_verification.notes: Added `*, include_frontier: bool = True` keyword-only parameter to assemble_tool_list in src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py. Verified by tests TestAC001IncludeFrontierKeywordOnly: parameter exists, default is True, kind is KEYWORD_ONLY, annotation is bool, positional pass raises TypeError.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: When `include_frontier=True`: the returned list includes `escalate_to_frontier` alongside all FEAT-J002 tools.
criteria_verification.evidence: Imported escalate_to_frontier from jarvis.tools.dispatch and conditionally include it in the returned list when include_frontier=True. Tests TestAC002IncludeFrontierTrue verify the escalation is present, list length is 10, all FEAT-J002 tools are present, and the order is alphabetical with escalate_to_frontier between dispatch_by_capability and get_calendar_events.
criteria_verification.notes: Imported escalate_to_frontier from jarvis.tools.dispatch and conditionally include it in the returned list when include_frontier=True. Tests TestAC002IncludeFrontierTrue verify the escalation is present, list length is 10, all FEAT-J002 tools are present, and the order is alphabetical with escalate_to_frontier between dispatch_by_capability and get_calendar_events.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: When `include_frontier=False`: the returned list **omits** `escalate_to_frontier` entirely and includes ALL other FEAT-J002 tools unchanged (scenario: *escalate_to_frontier is not present in the ambient tool list at all*; *the assembled list still contains all FEAT-JARVIS-002 tools*).
criteria_verification.evidence: When include_frontier=False the function returns the original FEAT-J002 9-tool list verbatim. Tests TestAC003IncludeFrontierFalse verify escalate_to_frontier is absent (by name and by object identity), length is 9, and the names match FEAT_J002_TOOL_NAMES exactly.
criteria_verification.notes: When include_frontier=False the function returns the original FEAT-J002 9-tool list verbatim. Tests TestAC003IncludeFrontierFalse verify escalate_to_frontier is absent (by name and by object identity), length is 9, and the names match FEAT_J002_TOOL_NAMES exactly.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: The returned list is a new list object each call (no mutable aliasing); the reasoning model cannot mutate a shared list to add `escalate_to_frontier` at runtime (scenario: *The reasoning model cannot add escalate_to_frontier to the ambient tool list at runtime* — ADR-ARCH-023).
criteria_verification.evidence: Both branches of the conditional return a fresh list literal on each call. Tests TestAC004NoMutableAliasing verify two consecutive calls return distinct list instances, attended and ambient calls return distinct instances, and a hostile append of escalate_to_frontier to a returned ambient list does not leak into a subsequent ambient call.
criteria_verification.notes: Both branches of the conditional return a fresh list literal on each call. Tests TestAC004NoMutableAliasing verify two consecutive calls return distinct list instances, attended and ambient calls return distinct instances, and a hostile append of escalate_to_frontier to a returned ambient list does not leak into a subsequent ambient call.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: No default coupling: `include_frontier` has no relationship to session state; it is a pure lifecycle-time flag set by the caller (`lifecycle.startup` in TASK-J003-015).
criteria_verification.evidence: The function never reads dispatch._current_session_hook or dispatch._async_subagent_frame_hook. Tests TestAC005NoSessionCoupling verify the flag is honoured both with no session resolver wired (True and False cases) and even when a fake attended session resolver is installed — the flag's value alone determines the surface.
criteria_verification.notes: The function never reads dispatch._current_session_hook or dispatch._async_subagent_frame_hook. Tests TestAC005NoSessionCoupling verify the flag is honoured both with no session resolver wired (True and False cases) and even when a fake attended session resolver is installed — the flag's value alone determines the surface.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Identity check: `escalate_to_frontier in assemble_tool_list(..., include_frontier=False)` is `False`; `... is True` when `include_frontier=True`.
criteria_verification.evidence: Tests TestAC006IdentityCheck use the imported escalate_to_frontier object (not just the string name) and assert membership matches the flag exactly: `escalate_to_frontier in result` is True when include_frontier=True and False when include_frontier=False.
criteria_verification.notes: Tests TestAC006IdentityCheck use the imported escalate_to_frontier object (not just the string name) and assert membership matches the flag exactly: `escalate_to_frontier in result` is True when include_frontier=True and False when include_frontier=False.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
criteria_verification.evidence: Code follows the existing module patterns: PEP 8 / Black-compatible formatting, type hints, comprehensive docstrings, and from __future__ import annotations. The new test file mirrors the conventions of tests/test_assemble_tool_list.py. All 196 tests across the touched modules pass with no warnings or errors attributable to this change.
criteria_verification.notes: Code follows the existing module patterns: PEP 8 / Black-compatible formatting, type hints, comprehensive docstrings, and from __future__ import annotations. The new test file mirrors the conventions of tests/test_assemble_tool_list.py. All 196 tests across the touched modules pass with no warnings or errors attributable to this change.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `the stack-specific Phase-3 specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.
BDD oracle: 3 scenario(s) reference step definitions that are not yet implemented. These do NOT block approval but should be implemented to make the scenarios runnable.
